records.ety: [OE. amonesten, OF. amonester, F. admonester, fr. a supposed LL. admonesstrare, fr. L. admonere to remind, warn; ad + monere to warn. See Monition.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Admonished (<?/); p. pr. & vb. n. Admonishing.]

defs: To warn or notify of a fault; to reprove gently or kindly, but seriously; to exhort.
To counsel against wrong practices; to cation or advise; to warn against danger or an offense; -- followed by of, against, or a subordinate clause.
quotes: Admonishing one another in psalms and hymns.
I warned thee, I admonished thee, foretold The danger, and the lurking enemy.

defs: To instruct or direct; to inform; to notify.
quotes: Moses was admonished of God, when he was about to make the tabernacle.
